content.problemStatement:
There are nnn chocolates, and you are given an array of nnn numbers where the iii-th number AiA_iAi​ is the flavour type of the iii-th chocolate. Sebrina wants to eat as many different types of chocolates as possible, but she also has to save at least xxx number of chocolates for her little brother.

Find the maximum possible number of distinct flavour types Sebrina can have.


Input Format
The first line contains an integer TTT --- the number of test cases.



The first line of each test case consists of two integers nnn, xxx - The number of chocolates Sabrina has and the number of chocolates she has to save for her brother, respectively.
The second line contains nnn integers A1,…,AnA_1,\ldots, A_nA1​,…,An​, where the iii-th chocolate has type AiA_iAi​.


Output Format
For each test case, output a single integer denoting the maximum possible number of distinct chocolate flavours Sabrina can eat.
constraints: Sum of nnn over all test cases do not exceed 2⋅1052 \cdot 10^5 2⋅105

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111: In the first case, the maximum number of chocolates Sebrina can have is 111 as she has to leave 111 chocolate for her brother. Thus, the maximum number of distinct chocolates is also 111.
Test case 222: Sebrina has to leave 222 chocolates for her brother. She can eat any 222 chocolates. Since all the chocolates have the same flavor, it does not matter which chocolates she eats. The maximum number of distinct chocolates would be 111.
Test case 333: Sebrina needs to save 333 chocolates for her brother. She can eat any 222 chocolates. To maximize the number of distinct chocolates, she can eat chocolates 111 and 555 with flavors 505050 and 100100100 respectively. Thus maximum number of distinct chocolates is 222.
